steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries question. The blank falls between the parenthetical defining endophytic fungi and the phrase "researching 120 endophytic fungi-produced compounds, she found..." The challenge is marking the boundary between two independent clauses: one ending after the parenthetical, and one beginning with the prepositional phrase "By researching..."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘plant). By’: A period correctly ends the first sentence after the parenthetical, and "By researching..." begins a new sentence with a prepositional phrase modifying the subject "she." This is a complete, grammatical boundary. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘plant), by’: A comma alone cannot join two independent clauses — this creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘plant) and by’: Without a comma before "and," the conjunction cannot join two sentences in this structure; the result is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘plant) by’: No punctuation between the two sentences fuses them into a run-on. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
